unit.name: InstructGPT, ChatGPT, and remaining risks
unit.description: Connects the algorithms to deployed assistants and discusses open-source DPO and alignment failures.
unit.summary: The final section connects the pipeline to practical assistant systems. InstructGPT is presented as the model family that defined the instruction-tuning plus RLHF pipeline, scaling toward roughly 30,000 tasks and using labeler data, reward modeling, and reinforcement learning [ts=4353s] [ts=4389s]. It improves on base GPT-3 by inferring user intent, such as explaining the moon landing to a six-year-old instead of continuing with unrelated questions [ts=4400s] [ts=4419s]. ChatGPT follows essentially the same core algorithmic pipeline but is optimized more specifically for dialogue and user interaction, with data quality described as the key practical game [ts=4436s] [ts=4462s]. DPO is described as important for open-source and production models because it is simpler and efficient; the instructor mentions Hugging Face leaderboards, Mistral, and Llama 3 [ts=4508s] [ts=4544s]. RLHF often produces more detailed and organized answers than SFT because humans prefer those properties [ts=4557s] [ts=4584s]. The lecture ends with warnings: reward hacking can optimize the wrong behavior, humans may prefer authoritative or long answers over truthful ones, and hallucinations do not disappear with RLHF [ts=4615s] [ts=4749s].

key_points.text: InstructGPT is presented as the first model family defining the SFT plus RLHF pipeline.
ChatGPT uses the same core algorithmic ideas but is optimized for dialogue.
DPO is described as widely useful in open-source and production post-training.
RLHF can make answers more detailed and better organized than SFT alone.
Reward hacking is illustrated by an agent repeatedly crashing a boat for points.
Preference optimization can favor authoritative or long answers over truthful or concise answers.

kp_catalog.name: InstructGPT and ChatGPT post-training pipeline
kp_catalog.description: The practical assistant-building pipeline and its remaining preference-alignment risks.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because instructGPT and ChatGPT post-training pipeline synthesizes the practical assistant-building pipeline and its remaining preference-alignment risks into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.

kp_catalog.name: Direct Preference Optimization
kp_catalog.description: A direct classification-style preference objective that avoids a separate RLHF optimization loop. DPO as a simpler preference-optimization method that avoids full PPO-style RL infrastructure.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because direct Preference Optimization formalizes how a direct classification-style preference objective that avoids a separate RLHF optimization loop, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context though roles split between capstone and gateway.

kp_catalog.name: Reward hacking and KL regularization
kp_catalog.description: The risk of exploiting learned rewards and the KL-style penalty that keeps the policy near the reference model. Optimizers exploiting incomplete reward specifications in reward-model-based alignment.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because reward hacking and KL regularization formalizes how the risk of exploiting learned rewards and the KL-style penalty that keeps the policy near the reference model, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.
